title: Citrusy Fluffy Sesame Pilaf
description: This festive Korean dish features a citrusy flavor profile, fluffy texture, and is prepared with sous-vide to ensure even cooking.
instructions: Preheat your sous-vide machine to 160°F (71°C). Rinse rice under cold water and soak it in a large pot for at least an hour.

Drain the soaked rice, then combine with one cup of water in another large pot. Bring to a boil over high heat, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until all liquid is absorbed and grains are tender, about 30 minutes. Let stand for 10 minutes before fluffing.

In a small skillet, toast sesame seeds over medium-high heat until lightly golden brown, about 2-3 minutes. Toast garlic cloves in the same pan until fragrant, about 1 minute, then add sliced green onions and cook briefly until they just begin to wilt.

Sauté onion in olive oil until translucent, about 5 minutes. Add minced ginger and continue cooking for another minute. Stir in soaked rice, toasted sesame seeds, and soy sauce. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Add lemon juice and tahini, stirring well to combine. Cook gently over low heat until slightly thickened, about 1-2 minutes. Season with salt to taste if desired.
Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and green onions before serving hot.
